channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Death toll in Gaza Strip from Israel-Hamas war tops 45,000, Palestinians say
item.description: Health officials in the Gaza Strip say the death toll from the 14-month war between Israel and Hamas militants has topped 45,000 people.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France rushes help to Mayotte, where hundreds or even thousands died in Cyclone Chido
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — France was rushing help by ship and military aircraft to its poor overseas territory of Mayotte in the Indian Ocean on Monday after the island was shattered by its worst storm in nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from Indonesian firing squad prepares to fly home
item.description: YOGYAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — A Filipina who was on death row in Indonesia and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015 will return home this week under an arrangement between the countries, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SLAMET RIYADI and NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For the first time in 8 years, a US Navy ship makes a port call in Cambodia, a top Chinese ally
item.description: A U.S. Navy warship has arrived in Cambodia, the first such visit in eight years to a nation that is China’s close ally in Southeast Asia.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Germany's Scholz faces a confidence vote. It's expected to lead to an election in February
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz faces a confidence vote in the German parliament on Monday that he's expected to lose, paving the way for the European Union's most populous member and biggest economy to hold an early election in February.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Trump threatens mass deportations, Central America braces for an influx of vulnerable migrants
item.description: SAN PEDRO SULA, Honduras (AP) — As dozens of deported migrants pack into a sweltering airport facility in San Pedro Sula, Norma sits under fluorescent lights clutching a foam cup of coffee and a small plate of eggs – all that was waiting for her in Honduras.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel closes its embassy in Ireland
item.description: Israel announced that it would close its embassy in Ireland on Sunday after the European country recognized Palestine as a sovereign nation and other acts that Israel said promoted “antisemitic rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russian paramilitary soldiers killed in friendly fire attack by North Koreans after enlisting DPRK help
item.description: North Korean troops recently killed Russian soldiers in a friendly fire attack, according to Ukrainian intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s impeached leader avoids investigators as court begins meeting to determine his fate
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol is dodging requests by investigative agencies to appear for questioning over his short-lived martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel will close its Ireland embassy over Gaza tensions as Palestinian death toll nears 45,000
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel said Sunday it will close its embassy in Ireland as relations deteriorated over the war in Gaza, where Palestinian medical officials said new Israeli airstrikes killed over 46 people including several children.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolivia's divisive popular vote for senior judges offers lessons for Mexico
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — Campaigning in Bolivia for Sunday's judicial election may be strictly forbidden, but look closer on the streets of the capital La Paz and you’ll find that some candidates have sneakily plastered their faces on packs of corn puffs and others have slipped subtle slogans into off
item.dc:creator: PAOLA FLORES and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: West Africa regional bloc approves exit timeline for 3 coup-hit member states
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — West Africa’s regional bloc ECOWAS approved Sunday an exit timeline for three coup-hit nations after a nearly yearlong process of mediation to avert the unprecedented disintegration of the grouping.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian President Lula discharged from hospital after surgery to stop brain bleed
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva was discharged from the hospital Sunday after undergoing surgery to stop a brain bleed.
item.dc:creator: THIAGO MOSTAZO and MAYCRON ABADE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-soccer player Kavelashvili becomes Georgia's president in a blow to country's EU aspirations
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili became president of Georgia on Saturday, as the ruling party tightened its grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US reiterates support for South Korea after impeachment of president
item.description: The United States reiterated its support for South Korea, an ally in East Asia, and vowed to advance mutual interests after the country’s president Yoon Suk Yeol was impeached by the parliament.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: A look at the whirlwind events that led to the impeachment of South Korea's president
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday voted to impeach embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived martial law decree this month, a shocking stunt that paralyzed the country’s politics, halted foreign policy and spooked financial markets.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey seeks to purge pro-US Kurdish force that helped defeat Islamic State in Syria
item.description: A Turkish strongman exploits chaos in Syria to oust pro-American Syrian Kurds from border area while solidifying expansion in war-torn nation
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia batters Ukraine power grid amid rising concern Putin could order ballistic missile attack this weekend
item.description: Ukraine's energy infrastructure was again pummeled in a "massive blow" by Russian missiles and drones on Friday, just days after the U.S. warned Russia might be planning another Oreshnik missile strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Who is Travis Timmerman, the missing American apparently found in Syria
item.description: An American man found wandering the streets outside the Syrian capital of Damascus barefoot identified himself as Pete Travis Timmerman on Thursday. Timmerman was last seen in Budapest, Hungary, in June and had not been heard from in months.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: NATO chief warns of 'huge risk' in Ukraine talks without Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte expressed concern about the idea of conducting peace negotiations in the nearly three-year-long Russia-Ukraine war without Russian President Vladimir Putin "at the table," calling the move "risky.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Biden readies travel bans on Georgia officials for undermining October election
item.description: President Biden is readying visa restrictions on officials in the country of Georgia amid a government crackdown on mass protests against the claimed victory by the Georgian Dream party, which critics say is turning the country toward Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau declares himself ‘proud feminist’ after lamenting Harris loss to Trump as setback for women
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday declared himself a "proud feminist" as he lamented Vice President Harris’ loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken testifies at hearing on Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Lawmakers have been demanding answers into how such a significant operation could have gone so wrong.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation
